atoms.subject: ctx:cancel-surface
atoms.label: Refund path computes 80% in BigDecimal at scale 2 HALF_EVEN and books the remainder explicitly

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.Order
atoms.object: status is set via the OrderStatus.NOTPAID.getCode() enum accessor at order creation, not a hardcoded integer literal, in both preserve services

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.OrderTicketsInfo
atoms.object: assurance field value 0 selects the no-assurance-purchase branch; a nonzero value selects the add-assurance call to the downstream assurance service

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.OrderTicketsInfo
atoms.object: a non-null and non-empty consigneeName field selects the consign-creation branch; empty or null selects the no-consign branch

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.OrderTicketsInfo
atoms.object: foodType field value 0 selects the no-food-order branch; a nonzero value selects the food-order-creation branch; value 2 additionally selects the stationName and storeName fields on the food order

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.Response
atoms.object: status 0 selects the early-return branch with null payload and the upstream message propagated, on checkSecurity/getContactsById/getTripAllDetailInformation/basicTravel calls in both preserve services

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.Response
atoms.object: status 1 selects the success branch on an optional sub-call (assurance/food/consign); any other value selects a fallback message without aborting the overall flow

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.SeatClass
atoms.object: seatType code equal to SeatClass.FIRSTCLASS code selects the confort-class seat-allocation branch; any other value selects the economy-class allocation branch

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.TravelResult
atoms.object: price value is obtained via a map lookup on getPrices() keyed by the seatclass label (confortClass or economyClass) and assigned directly to order.price with no visible parsing or rounding call at this call site

atoms.subject: ctx:inside-payment-surface
atoms.label: Inside-payment ledgers the shared scale-2 BigDecimal amount unchanged

atoms.subject: ctx:order-surface
atoms.label: Order keeps the price string but the shared reading is exact scale-2 BigDecimal

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.PreserveServiceImpl
atoms.object: account record looked up by accountId value appended as a REST path segment /users/id/{accountId}, not a query parameter or request-body field

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.PreserveServiceImpl
atoms.object: order.boughtDate and travel.departureTime fields populated from StringUtils.Date2String(new Date()) at request-handling time, not from client-supplied input

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.PreserveServiceImpl
atoms.object: sendEmail(notifyInfo, headers) is defined and would publish to the email queue via RabbitSend, but its call site in preserve() is commented out with a TODO change-to-async-message-service note, so the RabbitSend enqueue path is not reached on this code path

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.PreserveServiceImpl
atoms.object: new order status field set via OrderStatus.NOTPAID.getCode() enum accessor at order creation, not a raw integer literal at this site

atoms.object: seatType code equal to SeatClass.FIRSTCLASS.getCode() selects the firstclass seat-dispatch call and the confortClass price-map key; else selects the secondclass dispatch call and the economyClass price-map key

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.PreserveServiceImpl
atoms.object: response status 1 selects the success-log branch; else the overall order continues and the returned message degrades for the assurance/food/consign steps rather than aborting

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.PreserveServiceImpl
atoms.object: response status 0 selects the immediate-return branch carrying the upstream msg and null data, on checkSecurity/getContactsById/getTripAllDetailInformation/basicTravel/createOrder calls

atoms.object: account record looked up by accountId value appended as a REST path segment /users/id/{accountId}, not a query parameter or request-body field

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.PreserveOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: order.boughtDate and travel.departureTime fields populated from StringUtils.Date2String(new Date()) at request-handling time, not from client-supplied input

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.PreserveOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: sendEmail(notifyInfo, headers) is defined and would publish to the email queue via RabbitSend, but its call site in preserve() is commented out with a TODO change-to-async-message-service note, so the RabbitSend enqueue path is not reached on this code path

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.PreserveOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: new order status field set via OrderStatus.NOTPAID.getCode() enum accessor at order creation, not a raw integer literal at this site

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.PreserveOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: seatType code equal to SeatClass.FIRSTCLASS.getCode() selects the firstclass seat-dispatch call and the confortClass price-map key; else selects the secondclass dispatch call and the economyClass price-map key

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.PreserveOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: response status 1 selects the success-log branch; else the overall order continues and the returned message degrades for the assurance/food/consign steps rather than aborting

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.PreserveOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: response status 0 selects the immediate-return branch carrying the upstream msg and null data, on checkSecurity/getContactsById/getTripAllDetailInformation/basicTravel/createOrder calls

atoms.subject: ts-price-service.PriceConfig
atoms.object: id field is a 36-char String column populated from UUID.randomUUID().toString() in the no-arg constructor, and overwritten explicitly per seed row in InitData

atoms.subject: ts-price-service.PriceConfig
atoms.object: basicPriceRate and firstClassPriceRate are declared as primitive double persisted fields holding a multiplier rate (not a final ticket price amount); no string parsing or explicit rounding/scaling logic is present at the field-definition or seed-assignment sites

atoms.subject: ts-price-service.InitData
atoms.object: the seed row for routeId d693a2c5-... omits the setFirstClassPriceRate call, so — because the PriceConfig instance is reused across iterations — that field carries forward the value 1.0 set by the immediately preceding seed row rather than receiving a fresh value at this row

atoms.subject: ts-price-service.InitData
atoms.object: a single PriceConfig instance, created once, is reused and its fields reassigned for all ten seed calls rather than constructing a new instance per row

atoms.subject: ts-notification-service.application-yml
atoms.object: spring.mail.password falls back to the literal default GZKSCXHBOLKMCLDQ when the email_password env var is unset

atoms.subject: ts-notification-service.NotificationServiceImpl
atoms.object: boolean true selects the mail-dispatch-attempted branch (sendEmail did not throw), false selects the caught-exception branch with an error log, symmetric across all four notify methods

atoms.subject: ts-notification-service.NotificationServiceImpl
atoms.object: template=order_cancel_success.ftl selected for orderCancelSuccess; model reduced to username and price only (no startPlace/endPlace/startTime/date/seatClass/seatNumber)

atoms.subject: ts-notification-service.NotificationServiceImpl
atoms.object: template=order_changed_success.ftl selected for orderChangedSuccess; model shape (username/startPlace/endPlace/startTime/date/seatClass/seatNumber/orderNumber) identical to orderCreateSuccess

atoms.subject: ts-notification-service.NotificationServiceImpl
atoms.object: template=order_create_success.ftl selected for orderCreateSuccess; model adds orderNumber in place of preserveSuccess's price field

atoms.subject: ts-notification-service.NotificationServiceImpl
atoms.object: template=preserve_success.ftl selected for preserveSuccess; model carries username/startPlace/endPlace/startTime/date/seatClass/seatNumber/price

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.application-yml
atoms.object: spring.datasource.password falls back to the literal default root when the ORDER_OTHER_MYSQL_PASSWORD env var is unset

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.application-yml
atoms.object: the MySQL datasource block is active (uncommented) while the rabbitmq host/listener and cloud.stream binding block is fully commented out

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.Order
atoms.object: String price field defaults to the literal "0.0" in the no-arg constructor

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.Order
atoms.object: int status field defaults to OrderStatus.PAID.getCode() in the no-arg constructor

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherRepository
atoms.object: Order rows are looked up by primary key id, by accountId, or by the travelDate+trainNumber composite

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: Response envelope status=1 selects the result-carrying success branch, status=0 selects the not-found-or-rejected branch (data typically null or a sentinel), consistently across getSoldTickets/findOrderById/create/alterOrder/saveChanges

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: order.getPrice() String value is returned as-is in Response data with no parsing or arithmetic; getOrderPrice's not-found branch returns the literal string "-1.0"

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: when qi.isEnableStateQuery() is true, tempOrder.getStatus() is compared for exact equality against qi.getState() to select the statePassFlag-passing branch in queryOrders

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: orders with getStatus() >= OrderStatus.CHANGE.getCode() are excluded (continue) from the sold-ticket seat-class tally in queryAlreadySoldOrders

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.OrderOtherServiceImpl
atoms.object: in checkSecurityAboutOrder, an order is counted as valid (countTotalValidOrder incremented) when its status equals OrderStatus.NOTPAID, PAID, or COLLECTED code

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.QueryInfo
atoms.object: disableStateQuery() sets enableTravelDateQuery=false and state=-1, but never sets enableStateQuery=false

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.QueryInfo
atoms.object: enableStateQuery(int targetStatus) stores the argument in the state field, later read by OrderOtherServiceImpl.queryOrders as the order-status filter target

atoms.subject: ts-order-other-service.SecurityConfig
atoms.object: the broad permit-all matcher for /api/v1/orderOtherService/orderOther/** (line 74) is declared before the method-restricted matchers for the same base path (lines 75-79) -- opposite declaration order from ts-order-service.SecurityConfig

atoms.subject: ts-order-service.SecurityConfig
atoms.object: method-restricted matchers for POST/PUT/DELETE on /api/v1/orderservice/order and /admin (lines 75-79) are declared before the broad permit-all matcher for /api/v1/orderservice/order/** (line 80)

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-other-service.application-yml
atoms.object: listens on HTTP port 14569, registers with the nacos discovery cluster, and connects to RabbitMQ at a configurable host defaulting to localhost, port 5672

atoms.subject: ts-preserve-service.application-yml
atoms.object: listens on HTTP port 14568, registers with the nacos discovery cluster, and connects to RabbitMQ at a configurable host defaulting to localhost, port 5672

atoms.subject: ts-train-service.SecurityConfig
atoms.object: authorizeRequests chain lists the broad permit-all matcher for /api/v1/trainservice/** first, then lists the narrower admin-role-gated POST/PUT/DELETE trains matchers second, within a single antMatchers list evaluated in declaration order

atoms.subject: ts-travel-plan-service.TravelPlanServiceImpl
atoms.object: status-1 on the result-carrying branch, status-0 on the cannotFind/rejected branch, for getCheapest, getQuickest and getMinStation

atoms.subject: ts-travel-plan-service.TravelPlanServiceImpl
atoms.object: status-1 unconditionally on getTransferSearch, with no not-found or rejected branch

atoms.object: application.yml declares train-service, order-service, route-service, basic-service and seat-service base URL properties, but getServiceUrl(serviceName) builds provider URLs by concatenating "http://" with the literal service name rather than reading those yml properties

atoms.subject: ts-travel-service.TravelServiceImpl
atoms.object: status-1 on the result-carrying branch, status-0 on the not-found/no-content branch, for getRouteByTripId, getTrainTypeByTripId, getTripByRoute, retrieve, delete, and queryAll

atoms.subject: ts-travel-service.TravelServiceImpl
atoms.object: status-1 on both the newly-created branch and the tripId-already-exists branch of create()

atoms.subject: ts-travel-service.TravelServiceImpl
atoms.object: status-1 on both the updated branch and the tripId-not-found branch of update()

contexts.label: ts-common shared entity and envelope vocabulary

covers.label: money-amount representation convention across services exchanging price/refund/fee values (single shared section: money-amount)
order-status code vocabulary convention across services gating on order state (single shared section: order-status-code)
global structural surface: all measured service contexts and the shared vocabulary
Source-grounded finite settlement complex with explicit pair and triple intersections
Observed settlement surface for law evaluation
